# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

broker_id: <BROKER-ID> # provide a unique id per instance of the kafka server, recommended to be same as zk name if you are using one or more Zookeeper servers for clustering purposes   
zookeeper.connect: "<ZOOKEEPER1-HOSTNAME>:2181"  # provide hostname and port where your primary/leader kafka broker is running  
broker_address: <BROKER-ADDRESS>  # Internal IP or DNS name if you want to use it for clients, can be null (only with Zookeeper)   
port: 9092                                     # Default port used by Kafka brokers     
log.dirs: "/tmp/kafka"                        # Directory under which the broker stores log files    
zookeeper_connection_timeout_ms : "1500",   # Time in ms to wait before giving up initial connection attempt for Zookeepers   
replication_factor: 3                         # The replication factor allows Kafka brokers replicate data across multiple nodes, it's also used by consumers as a limit on the total bytes delivered per message. Must be at least two and must not exceed 'max.total.bytes'. Default is three    
log4j_options: "-XX:+UseG1GC -Xmx500M"   # JVM arguments for log gc tuning, change as needed
